reviews.why: True homonym, all refs CAKE. r1 ("food and beverage costs were 23.4% vs 24.6%... menu price increases in excess of inflation") and r3 ("COGS were a helper with that flat food cost") name the company-wide F&B/COGS cost line driven by input-cost levels vs menu pricing. r2 ("new restaurants... actual-to-theoretical food cost inefficiencies") names operational execution inefficiency at new units during ramp-up. The 3-check fails on all three: different metric (cost ratio vs actual-to-theoretical variance), different scope (company-wide vs new restaurants), different mechanism (input prices/pricing vs kitchen execution). Names coined only from evidence words; both verified absent from existing_seed_names (near-duplicates of the food_beverage_costs cluster and food_efficiency are intentional — dedup links them later; collision avoided).

split names failed mini-gate (REJECT both. (1) dining_experience_demand — "dining_experience" is a vague/invented phrase, not a concrete thing, actor, or standard financial term (violates R3 word-order anchor of concrete thing->metric, R4 ban on invented synonyms/vague phrases, R7 ban on vague descriptors too broad to name a causal variable). It is also not clearly distinct from the sibling name — casual dining IS a dining experience — so the homonym split stays ambiguous (R10 last bullet: unresolved ambiguity -> reject). The ontology's own example for this domain is restaurant_traffic. (2) casual_dining_industry_demand — "industry" is a redundant scope/category filler that adds no causal content (R7 category-word ban, R9 only include parts the evidence attributes, R8 keep names short); stripping it gives the canonical form casual_dining_demand, the exact name being split, so under the one-name-per-driver canonicalization rule (§2) this proposal collapses back into the original homonym and resolves nothing. Both names pass mechanical format checks (lowercase snake, no tickers, no state/direction/period tokens), but vagueness and canonical-collapse fail the gate, so all_admit=false.) — parked fail-close: True homonym: r1/r2 quote "resilient consumer demand for the... dining experiences we provide our guests" and r3 (own comp sales rising despite a softening environment) name demand for the company's own dining concepts — company scope; r2 explicitly contrasts it against "the broader casual dining industry". r4 ("casual dining demand has had more macro sensitivity versus... QSRs") names demand for the casual-dining industry segment — industry scope. Different object and scope fail the 3-check, so the meanings are split. Both new names are absent from existing_seed_names (near neighbors dining_demand, consumer_dining_demand, experiential_dining_demand, casual_dining_industry_sales/traffic exist, so distinguishing evidence words "experience" and "industry" were added).

True homonym: the record unions reported capex actuals (r4/r5/r6/r8 10-Q "Capital expenditures ... were $X million"; r9/r10 transcript "CapEx totaled approximately $X million") with forward capex guidance (r1/r2/r3/r7 "We anticipate approximately $X to $Y million in [cash] capital expenditures"). Same metric and scope (CAKE capex for unit development + maintenance) but different mechanism — reported actual vs forward outlook — failing check 3; the ontology's earnings convention treats {metric}_guidance as a separate coined driver. Natural names (capital_expenditures, capex, capital_expenditure, capital_expenditure_guidance, capex_guidance) all already exist in the seed, so I coined more-specific evidence-word variants: cash_capital_expenditure_guidance ("cash capital expenditures" verbatim in r1/r2/r7) for the guidance refs, and restaurant_capital_expenditures ("for new restaurants", "maintenance on our restaurants") taking the remaining actuals refs.

True homonym, flag confirmed. r1 (61.0M-share authorization, 1.1M remaining) and r2 (program has no expiration, no required purchases, modifiable/terminable) both describe the board's authorization — its size and terms, a capacity/permission object. r3 (repurchased 0.6M shares for $21.7M) is executed buyback activity — a different metric (shares/dollars actually bought) with a different mechanism (capital deployment reducing float vs board signal of capacity). Same-object and same-mechanism checks fail, so the refs cannot stay under one name. Natural names share_repurchase_authorization and share_repurchases already exist in the seed, so distinguishing evidence words were added: board_share_repurchase_authorization ("Board of Directors has authorized") and shares_repurchased ("repurchased 0.6 million shares"). Both names are absent from existing_seed_names (no collision); they are near-duplicates of the existing drivers the flag pointed to, which dedup can link later.
